item.text: I’m using the `proposal-review` skill because the request explicitly invokes it. I’ll keep this bounded to the inline proposal idea and project-local artifacts, with no file edits.

item.aggregated_output:
# Customer Fixture Workflow Guide

## Customer-project portability

Public skills operate in customer-project mode by default.

Use project-local artifacts when present and relevant.

Do not require RigorLoop repository-internal specs, docs, root governance, reports, or follow-up files in this customer fixture.

When local guidance is absent, use portable defaults where safe and block on ambiguity where no safe default exists.

## Artifact locations

| Artifact | Location |
|---|---|
| specs | `specs/` |
| plans | `docs/plans/` |
| change root | `docs/changes/<change-id>/` |

item.text: The workflow guide is relevant because the idea mentions documenting behavior in the local spec, and it provides the local artifact-location convention. I’m doing one quick code/test check only to ground the current `greeting(name)` behavior.

- evidence used: inline proposal idea; `docs/workflows.md`; `src/app.js`; `tests/app.test.js`
- whether local `docs/workflows.md` was used: yes
- portable default or ambiguity behavior: portable default supports documenting local spec under `specs/`; ambiguity remains around API shape, default behavior, and allowed punctuation suffix values
- attempted reliance on absent RigorLoop internals: no
- review status: changes-requested
- top concern: define the contract explicitly, especially preserving `greeting(name) -> "hello <name>"` while specifying how the optional suffix is passed and validated
